summary: Joel introduces a series on functional specifications, noting that his Joel Test revealed specs as a major pain point for developers. He draws an analogy between writing specs and flossing — universally acknowledged as important but rarely practiced. The post serves as a teaser for a multi-part deep dive into functional specifications, starting with making the case for why they matter.
key_points: Writing specs was one of the biggest sore points revealed by The Joel Test
There is a universal gap between knowing specs are important and actually writing them
The series promises to cover the what, why, and how of functional specifications
money_quotes: It seems that specs are like flossing: everybody knows they should be writing them, but nobody does.
key_insight: The gap between knowing you should write specs and actually doing it is one of the most common failures in software development.
